event: PLA begins shelling Kinmen (Quemoy) islands
notes: Opening bombardment of First Taiwan Strait Crisis; PLA shelled Kinmen and nearby islands

event: PLA bombs Yijiangshan Islands
notes: Aerial bombing campaign against Nationalist-held Dachen and Yijiangshan islands

event: PLA captures Yijiangshan Islands
notes: First amphibious assault; ROC garrison overwhelmed; 720 defenders killed or captured

event: US Congress passes Formosa Resolution
notes: Authorized President Eisenhower to defend Taiwan and Penghu; implied defense of offshore islands

event: ROC evacuates Dachen Islands (Operation Jingang)
notes: US 7th Fleet assisted evacuation of 28000 civilians and military from Dachen Islands

event: Ceasefire ends First Taiwan Strait Crisis
notes: PLA ceases shelling; crisis de-escalates after US nuclear threats and diplomatic pressure

event: PLA begins massive bombardment of Kinmen (823 Artillery Bombardment)
notes: 44000 shells fired on first day; approximately 474000 shells over 44 days targeting Kinmen

event: US Secretary Dulles issues Newport Declaration
notes: Declared US would not retreat from defending offshore islands; signaled nuclear deterrence

event: US Navy escorts ROC supply convoys to Kinmen
notes: US 7th Fleet provided escort for resupply convoys; demonstrated commitment to defense

event: PLA announces ceasefire on even-numbered days
notes: Bizarre alternate-day shelling pattern; de facto end of active crisis phase

event: Second Taiwan Strait Crisis effectively ends
notes: PLA shifts to symbolic odd-day shelling only; continued sporadically until 1979

event: PLA conducts first missile tests near Taiwan
notes: DF-15 missiles fired into waters 150km north of Taiwan in response to President Lee Teng-hui US visit

event: PLA conducts second round of missile exercises
notes: Additional missile tests and live-fire naval exercises in Taiwan Strait

event: PLA launches missiles bracketing Taiwan (M-9/DF-15)
notes: Three DF-15 missiles landed in target zones near Keelung and Kaohsiung ports; major escalation

event: PLA begins large-scale military exercises (Exercise Strait 961)
notes: Combined arms exercises simulating amphibious invasion; 150000 troops involved

event: US deploys USS Nimitz and USS Independence carrier groups
notes: Largest US naval deployment in Asia since Vietnam War; two carrier battle groups transited Taiwan Strait

event: Third Taiwan Strait Crisis de-escalates
notes: PLA exercises conclude; Lee Teng-hui wins presidential election on March 23

event: PRC tests anti-satellite weapon (ASAT)
notes: SC-19 missile destroyed Fengyun-1C satellite at 865km altitude; demonstrated space warfare capability

event: PRC declares East China Sea ADIZ
notes: Unilateral Air Defense Identification Zone over Senkaku/Diaoyu area; raised regional tensions

event: PLA J-11 fighters cross Taiwan Strait median line
notes: First deliberate crossing of informal median line by PLA fighters since 1999

event: PLA aircraft conduct large-scale ADIZ incursion (18 aircraft)
notes: Record incursion at the time; included H-6 bombers with escort fighters

event: PLA record 38 aircraft enter Taiwan ADIZ in single day
notes: PRC National Day provocation; largest single-day incursion recorded at that time

event: PLA 56 aircraft enter Taiwan ADIZ (new record)
notes: Four-day surge totaling 149 sorties; included nuclear-capable H-6 bombers and J-16 fighters

event: US Speaker Nancy Pelosi visits Taiwan
notes: Highest-ranking US official to visit Taiwan since Speaker Gingrich in 1997

event: PLA launches unprecedented military exercises around Taiwan
notes: Live-fire exercises in six zones surrounding Taiwan; five DF-15 ballistic missiles overflew Taiwan

event: PLA ballistic missiles fly over Taiwan for first time
notes: Japan MOD confirmed five missiles landed in Japan EEZ; first missiles to overfly Taiwan island

event: PLA extends military exercises beyond original schedule
notes: Exercises extended without announced end date; simulated sea and air blockade operations

event: PLA concludes major exercises but announces regular patrols
notes: PLA declared exercises concluded but announced routine patrols would continue near median line

event: PLA sends 71 aircraft and 7 naval vessels near Taiwan (record)
notes: Largest single-day military activity; response to US defense spending bill including Taiwan provisions

event: PLA launches Joint Sword exercise around Taiwan
notes: Three-day exercise simulating encirclement; response to President Tsai Ing-wen meeting with US Speaker McCarthy

event: Joint Sword exercise concludes
notes: Exercise included simulated precision strikes and blockade operations from multiple directions

event: PLA deploys record 103 military aircraft near Taiwan
notes: Largest single-detection of PLA aircraft; included fighters and bombers crossing median line

event: PLA launches Joint Sword-2024A exercises
notes: Two-day exercise surrounding Taiwan following President Lai Ching-te inauguration on May 20

event: Joint Sword-2024A exercises conclude
notes: Exercises simulated joint seizure of battlefield control; practiced blockade of key ports and areas

event: PLA launches Joint Sword-2024B exercises
notes: One-day large-scale exercise surrounding Taiwan; response to President Lai National Day speech

event: PLA deploys record naval and coast guard vessels around Taiwan
notes: Included coast guard vessels enforcing simulated law enforcement operations; new dimension of coercion

event: PLA conducts large-scale naval exercise in western Pacific
notes: Combined fleet exercise involving Shandong carrier group east of Taiwan; demonstrated far-seas capability
